title: note_g_d column/field name is wrong — stores diversifier d, not g_d curve point
description: The DB column, migration comment, struct field, and struct doc all say 'g_d' (the derived Jubjub curve point, 32+ bytes). The field actually stores the raw 11-byte diversifier d. PaymentAddress::from_bytes([d(11)|pk_d(32)]) is correct code, but the name is wrong. When someone writes the compact-block scanner and reads 'note_g_d BLOB — Sapling diversifier g_d = GH(diversifier_bytes)', they may store the 32-byte g_d curve point, causing NoteDeserialize('must be 11 bytes') errors and hours of debugging. The column name is now in the SQLite schema permanently. Fix: rename to note_diversifier everywhere (migration v7 with ALTER TABLE RENAME COLUMN if supported, or a data migration).
close_reason: note_g_d renamed to note_diversifier in migration DDL, SpendableNote struct, SQL queries, and all tests across db.rs and tx_builder.rs
